items.headline: City commissions set 2026-27 priorities including traffic and parks
items.summary: Saratoga City Council approved work plans for five commissions covering heritage preservation, library/public art, parks and recreation, traffic safety, and planning. The Traffic Safety Commission's priorities could affect commuting and road conditions throughout the city.

items.headline: City launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: Cupertino is offering a business license amnesty program and conducting outreach to shopping centers, likely to help previously non-compliant businesses get licensed. The city is also rolling out a "Summer of Celebrations" shop local campaign and new economic development incentives for retail and startups.

items.headline: Capitol Dental Office opening on North Capitol Avenue
items.summary: A new $500,000 dental practice is opening at 585 N Capitol Av, bringing dental services to the area. This is the only significant commercial project in this permit batch.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up approved as annual city event
items.summary: City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored special event. The city will use city funds and accept donated materials and services to support the cleanup initiative.

items.headline: City presents federal housing grant funding opportunities
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed applications for federal Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These grants support affordable housing and community development projects, making them relevant to residents interested in how Sunnyvale addresses housing affordability.

items.headline: Palo Alto considers retail vitality policy changes to zoning code
items.summary: The Planning Commission is reviewing proposed amendments to zoning and building regulations designed to implement retail vitality policies from the city's Comprehensive Plan. This could affect where and how retail businesses operate in Palo Alto.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan study explores land use and transportation
items.summary: The Planning Commission held a study session on potential land use and transportation alternatives for the San Antonio Road area. This planning effort could shape how the corridor develops and how residents and traffic move through that part of the city.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The City Council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance on March 24, which modifies regulations for residents keeping bees. The changes affect what Campbell residents can and cannot do with backyard beekeeping.
